title: CLI writes UTC (Z) timestamps; ledger convention is +03:00 offset
resolution: Fixed: timestamps centralized in src/core/time.ts (nowIso/idStamp) and standardized on LOCAL machine time with numeric offset (e.g. 2026-07-06T02:32:50+03:00), per owner decision. mutate.ts uses the helper. Verified via a dogfooded claim/finish. Existing UTC-Z records from earlier this session remain valid ISO and were left as-is.
description: The mutation commands stamp records/events via `new Date().toISOString()`, which emits UTC with a trailing `Z` (e.g. 2026-07-05T23:13:33.686Z). The hand-authored ledger uses local offset format (e.g. 2026-07-06T12:24:00+03:00). Both are valid ISO-8601 and sort correctly, so this is cosmetic, not a correctness bug.
expected: Timestamps written by the CLI use a consistent, project-standard format.
actual: CLI-written timestamps are UTC `Z`; hand-authored ones are +03:00.
next_steps: Decide the canonical timestamp format (recommend: always UTC Z everywhere, and normalize the hand-authored records during task-migrate-ledger-json).
Centralize timestamp creation in one core helper so CLI/dashboard/MCP agree.
